channel.title: china-static:us
channel.description: Static RSS merge for china variant (us)

item.title: Mamdani's political gamble pays off as his endorsed candidates sweep their primaries
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: All three progressive candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani — including two who took on Democratic incumbents — won their primaries in safe seats almost guaranteeing their election in November.

item.title: Supreme Court rules that prison guards can't be sued for shaving Rastafarian's head
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.

item.title: In symbolic vote, Congress directs Trump to remove forces from Iran war
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: The measure to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran does not require the president's signature, nor does it carry the force of law. But it reflects bipartisan frustration with the war.

item.title: Congress passes the largest housing affordability bill in decades
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: An expansive bipartisan bill intended to bring down the cost of housing by boosting the supply of homes has passed both houses of Congress, and is headed to the president's desk for a signature.

item.title: Is AI 'one big bubble'? Behind the tech sell-off
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."

item.title: Portugal's Ronaldo shakes off World Cup doubters, scores 2 after sluggish start
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.

item.title: A Revolutionary War soldier's DNA links him to living relatives
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: Continental Army soldier John Pumphrey enlisted as a teenager in 1777 and fought at significant battles before his death in action against the British in Camden, S.C.

item.title: Afghan Taliban hold first, closed-door talks with EU on deportations
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: Rights groups criticized the meeting, saying it undermines the EU's human rights obligations.

item.title: U.S. lifts Iran oil sanctions. And, federal judge rules SAVE voter tool unlawful
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.

item.title: Who would pay for Trump's proposed $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund?
item.dc:creator: NPR
item.description: It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."

item.title: Trump pleads not guilty to 34 felony counts
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: Haberman reveals why Trump attacked judge and his family in speech
item.dc:creator: CNN
item.description: CNN political contributor Maggie Haberman explains the reasoning behind Donald Trump's attacks on the judge and his family during a speech at his Mar-a-Lago resort after he was arraigned on felony charges.

item.title: What to know about the Trump indictment on the eve of his court appearance
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: READ: Trump indictment related to hush money payment
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: Russian authorities detain suspect over St. Petersburg cafe blast
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: Video shows moment of deadly explosion at cafe in Russia
item.dc:creator: CNN
item.description: A well-known Russian military blogger, Vladlen Tatarsky, was killed in an explosion at a cafe in St. Petersburg, authorities said. CNN's Matthew Chance reports.

item.title: Wall Street Journal reporter Evan Gershkovich appeals against arrest, Russian state media says
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: Suspected Chinese spy balloon was able to transmit information back to Beijing
item.dc:creator: CNN
item.description: The suspected Chinese spy balloon that transited the US earlier this year was able to capture imagery and collect some signals intelligence from US military sites, a source familiar with the matter tells CNN.

item.title: Beijing promised to 'fight back' over Taiwan leader's US visit. But this time it has more to lose
item.dc:creator: CNN
item.description: An anticipated meeting between Taiwan's President Tsai Ing-wen and US House Speaker Kevin McCarthy in California this week has sparked concerns of a repeat of the pressure campaign China launched last year when then-Speaker Nancy Pelosi visited Taipei.

item.title: 'Scary, cold, hungry and lonely': Volunteer soldier shares experience on front line
item.dc:creator: CNN
item.description: A young volunteer soldier, Akula, who signed up for the war at the beginning of the Russian invasion is now dealing with the trauma of war. He spoke to CNN's David McKenzie about his time on the front lines and how it has affected his mental health.

item.title: Trump’s Iran endgame grates on Hill Republicans
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: Many lawmakers want more details on war-ending talks before delivering billions of dollars in new Pentagon spending.

item.title: Lutnick privately warned top executives of possible action against imported Chinese robots
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: The Commerce secretary told executives in a closed-door meeting that the department is reviewing state-subsidized robotics imports as the U.S. races China.

item.title: ‘No one wants to talk about Iran’: White House hopes to pivot back to economy
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: The administration is trying to reframe the conversation while batting down the critics.

item.title: Senate votes to halt Iran war despite Trump’s push for peace deal
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: Both the Senate and House have approved anti-war measures, dealing a stinging political blow to Trump.

item.title: Hormuz evacuation plans emerge after months of gridlock
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: A global effort to create an evacuation lane includes the International Maritime Organization, U.S., Iran, Oman and other countries.

item.title: Left-wing insurgents storm New York City
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: Mamdani-backed candidates toppled establishment Democrats and deepened pressure on Chuck Schumer, Hakeem Jeffries and the party’s old guard.

item.title: Cait Conley wins Democratic primary in blow to Rep. Mike Lawler
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: The Army vet will now try to oust the Republican incumbent in a district that favored Kamala Harris in 2024.

item.title: Rep. April McClain Delaney wins bitter primary to keep her Maryland House seat
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: Her victory in the Democratic contest for the 6th District blocks former Rep. David Trone from returning to Congress.

item.title: Poll: UK voters wanted Starmer gone, but not an immediate Burnham coronation
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: Results from The POLITICO Poll suggest that while U.K. voters were eager for new leadership, they were less interested in a shortcut for selecting the country’s new leader.

item.title: Judge vacates Trump policy allowing arrests at immigration courts
item.dc:creator: Politico
item.description: The Trump administration changed detention guidelines last year, causing a spike in courthouse arrests.
